rows.description: Item de Solestis varijs
rows.notes: Clear centered page heading in larger script with no amounts to the right; unambiguous as a structural label.

rows.description: Acct dinner
rows.notes: Description legible. Right-hand three columns clearly show '02' (pounds), '18' (shillings), '6' (pence). Vertical ruling lines are clear here and values are within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Dinner for college/accounting purposes

rows.description: Jennins taxe for Michl. 1708.
rows.notes: Text reasonably legible as 'Jennins taxe for Michl. 1708'. Amount columns read as '00' pounds, '18' shillings, '0' pence; shillings '18' is clear though the pence digit is faint, chosen as 0 based on alignment with column and nearby rows.
rows.english_description: Tax paid to Jennings at Michaelmas 1708

rows.description: For a common prayer book for ye chapel
rows.notes: Description readable. Right-hand shows '00' pounds, '16' shillings, '8' pence; digits align well with ruling lines and are distinct.
rows.english_description: Purchase of a common prayer book for the chapel

rows.description: To James Davys for work and materials
rows.notes: Name and phrase legible. Amount columns read '00', '10', '6' though the pence '6' is slightly smudged; alignment and neighbouring rows support reading.
rows.english_description: Payment to James Davys for work and materials

rows.description: To Goodw. Day for hooks to fasten ye trees in the back yard
rows.notes: Long description readable. Amount appears as '02' pounds, '09' shillings, '6' pence; the '9' shillings is clear though the pence loop is slightly inked.
rows.english_description: Payment to Goodwife Day for hooks to secure trees in the back yard

rows.description: To Jo. Warren of Nethercott by consent
rows.notes: Name and phrase clear. Amount columns read '00' '05' '0'; pence zero is inferred from a blank/short mark in pence column aligned with the zero in similar rows.
rows.english_description: Payment to John Warren of Nethercott by consent

rows.description: Butchery books Natv. 18? Bapt. 19? 6?
rows.notes: Description is cramped and partially abbreviated (Natv. / Bapt.). Right-hand shows '01' pounds, '17' shillings, '6' pence but the middle annotation numbers in the description are unclear; confidence lower because of ambiguity in the text though numeric columns align.
rows.english_description: Butchery account books for Christmas and Baptism services

rows.description: To stubs for work in ye back yard
rows.notes: Description clear. Amounts read as '01' '17' '6' — the pounds and shillings especially align with vertical ruling; pence digit slightly faint but consistent with adjacent totals.
rows.english_description: Payment for stubs (materials) for work in the back yard

rows.description: To mr Levys Widow by consent
rows.notes: Description legible though the name's spelling is abbreviated. Amount registers as '00' '09' '0' with clear alignment; pence column is a small blank/zero mark consistent across the page.
rows.english_description: Allowance paid to Mr Levy's widow by consent

rows.description: To mr Pain ye Brazier
rows.notes: Description clear. Numeric columns show '00' pounds, '10' shillings, '00' pence (pence column appears as two small strokes indicating zero).
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Pain, the brazier

rows.description: To Frogley Apothecary by consent
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount read as '00' '05' '0'; pence faint but consistent with a blank/zero mark aligned in pence column.
rows.english_description: Payment to Frogley, the apothecary, by consent

rows.description: For many baggs 2d. 2d. 2d
rows.notes: Description mentions several '2d' items. Amount columns show '00' pounds, '10' shillings, '0' pence; the shillings '10' is clear but the description is a little cramped and the relationship between the small '2d' marks and the amount is interpretive.
rows.english_description: Purchase of many bags

rows.description: To Hellicar ye Glasier
rows.notes: Description readable. Numeric columns appear as '00' pounds, '00' shillings, '6' pence — the pounds and shillings columns are blank/zero; pence '6' is visible but slightly smudged.
rows.english_description: Payment to Hellicar the glazier

rows.description: To Lumley ye Plumer
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount reads as '00' pounds, '02' shillings, '01' pence; the pence digit is small but aligns with the pence column.
rows.english_description: Payment to Lumley the plumber

rows.description: Subscriber for Dr Potters Clemens Alexander
rows.notes: Description is cramped and partly crossed by a small mark; numeric columns show '00' '06' '8' but the pence digit is somewhat ambiguous (could be 8 or a 6). Chosen 8 based on alignment with the pence column relative to adjacent lines.
rows.english_description: Subscription payment for Dr Potter's 'Clemens Alexander'

rows.description: To mr Subcret Adams for his going to Little Tue
rows.notes: Description partly abbreviated ('Subcret' for Sub-rector?) and place name contracted. Amount reads '00' '04' '0' with clear shillings '4' and a blank/zero pence mark.
rows.english_description: Expense paid to Mr Adams for his trip to Little Tew

rows.description: Candles { to Cosins Ann. 4. 19. 0 mich. 4. 15. 0 } to Broadwater & anno
rows.notes: Complex, bracketed line listing several distributions with internal shorthand. The right-hand shows '00' pounds, '09' shillings and what appears to be '14' in the pence column — this violates the pence (0–11) rule and indicates column confusion. Because of that, I interpreted the visible trailing '14' as actually shillings (i.e., '09' may belong to one sub-item). Due to severe ambiguity in how the amounts align with the internal bracketed notes, confidence is low. (Kept numbers to show a captured value but this row likely needs re-alignment.)
rows.english_description: Candles provided to the Cosins at Annunciation and Michaelmas and to Broadwater annually

rows.description: To mr Bugg by consent
rows.notes: Name and phrase legible. Amount columns read '00' '06' '9' though the pence is slightly faint; chosen based on vertical alignment with ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Bugg by consent

rows.description: To Cocus for rams, Newyears day, and 12th day
rows.notes: Description partially faded (name looks like 'Cocus' or 'Cogus'). Amount area shows '00' pounds, '07' shillings, '05' pence with a faint loop after the pence digit that resembles the 'ob' halfpenny mark. Because the fraction mark is faint and the name uncertain, confidence is below 0.5.
rows.english_description: Payment to the cook for rams supplied for New Year's Day and Twelfth Day

rows.description: To Cocus for sewell, Oatmeal, salt &c
rows.notes: Description legible as supplies. Columns read '04' pounds, '06' shillings, '00' pence. Pounds '4' is relatively large and clearly written; shillings legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to the cook for suet, oatmeal, salt and other provisions

rows.description: Laid out on ye new buildings by moneys taken out of ye treasury
rows.notes: Long descriptive entry clearly referring to building expenses. Numeric columns read '44' (pounds), '17' (shillings), '10' (pence). All values within valid ranges and clearly aligned with the vertical ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Expenditure on the new buildings paid out of the treasury

rows.description: Sax for Backgate { To Jennings To George }
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and contains an internal brace listing 'To Jennings' and 'To George'. Amount columns show '00' '16' '8' though some ink smudging near the braces reduces confidence.
rows.english_description: Sacks for Backgate supplied to Jennings and George

rows.description: To mr Sherwin for bringing ladlane and Medenham rent
rows.notes: Description legible but cramped. Amount read as '00' '16' '8' but digits are faint and the same '16 8' pattern appears twice on adjacent lines, raising the possibility of misalignment; confidence moderate-low.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Sherwin for bringing in the Ladlane and Medenham rents

rows.description: To mr Dodwell for keeping court at Little Tue
rows.notes: Entry text clear. Right-hand amounts read '00' pounds, '02' shillings, '06' pence with clear digit shapes in shillings and pence columns.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Dodwell for holding court at Little Tew

rows.description: To Goodw. Day for 3 doz of hooks for ye trees in ye back yard
rows.notes: Description reads like a repeat/related item to earlier Goodwife Day entry. Amount columns show '01' pounds, '00' shillings, '00' pence though pence area has a faint mark that could be fractional; chosen zero because no clear fraction symbol present.
rows.english_description: Payment to Goodwife Day for three dozen hooks for trees in the back yard

rows.description: Wine for ye 5th of Octbr
rows.notes: Short description; amounts read '00' '07' '6' with digits clear and properly aligned.
rows.english_description: Wine purchased for the fifth of October

rows.description: To mr Clarke for ink, pens and paper
rows.notes: Description clear. Amount column shows '00' pounds, '01' shilling, '03' pence and a small trailing loop in the pence area interpreted as an 'ob' halfpenny (0.5). The fraction mark is faint but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Clarke for ink, pens and paper (stationery)

rows.description: Pewrent charges in part
rows.notes: Description partly smudged at start but reads as 'Pewrent charges in part'. Amount read as '00' '03' '03' (three shillings three pence). The pence digits are faint; no fraction mark visible.
rows.english_description: Part payment of pew-rent charges

rows.description: Total (final line)
rows.notes: Final total in the right margin clearly written as '142' (pounds), '3' (shillings), '8' (pence). Numerals are bold and aligned with ruling lines; high confidence.
